title: Modal missing ARIA attributes for screen readers
description: The custom path modal dialog lacks proper ARIA attributes: role='dialog', aria-modal='true', and aria-labelledby. This makes the modal inaccessible to screen reader users.
recommendation: Add: role='dialog' aria-modal='true' aria-labelledby='modal-title' and an id='modal-title' on the h2 element

title: Edit modal missing ARIA attributes
description: The edit path modal uses AnimatePresence/motion.div but lacks role='dialog', aria-modal='true', and aria-labelledby attributes for proper screen reader support.
recommendation: Add role='dialog' aria-modal='true' and aria-labelledby pointing to the modal title

title: Modal lacks keyboard navigation support
description: Pressing Escape should close the modal, and focus should be trapped within the modal when open. Currently neither behavior exists.
recommendation: Add useEffect with keydown listener for Escape key and implement focus trap using useRef

title: Modal lacks keyboard navigation and focus management
description: The edit modal doesn't close on Escape key and doesn't manage focus properly when opening/closing.
recommendation: Add onKeyDown handler for Escape and use autoFocus on the first input when modal opens

title: Skills badges use div instead of semantic list
description: The skills tags are rendered as plain divs with map(), but semantically should be a ul/li list for screen readers.
recommendation: Wrap in <ul> and use <li> for each skill tag

title: Channel badges use plain span without semantic list
description: Channel tags are rendered with div.map() which isn't semantically correct for a list of items.
recommendation: Use <ul>/<li> semantics for channel badges

title: Difficulty filter doesn't handle 'Beginner Friendly'
description: The CuratedPath interface allows 'Beginner Friendly' as a difficulty value (line 17 in learning-paths-data.ts), but the filter only checks for exact match 'Beginner'. GenZHomePage.tsx uses 'Beginner Friendly' but it won't appear in filtered results.
recommendation: Update filter logic: path.difficulty === activeTab || (activeTab === 'Beginner' && path.difficulty.includes('Beginner'))

title: Invalid channel reference 'docker' doesn't exist
description: The DevOps curated path references 'docker' as a channel, but this channel ID doesn't exist in allChannelsConfig. Users clicking on this path will encounter broken links or missing content.
recommendation: Remove 'docker' from DevOps channels array, or add docker channel to channels-config.ts if intended

title: Channel IDs displayed instead of names
description: Custom paths store channel IDs (like 'algorithms', 'frontend') but MyPath.tsx displays them raw without resolving to human-readable names. Users see 'algorithms' instead of 'Algorithms'.
recommendation: Create a lookup map from allChannelsConfig: const channelName = allChannelsConfig.find(c => c.id === channel)?.name || channel

title: activePathId state is redundant and buggy
description: The component tracks activePathId in state, but isPathActive() reads from localStorage directly. This causes inconsistency - after toggling, setActivePathId is set to updatedPaths[0] which may not reflect the user's selection if they activated a different path first.
recommendation: Remove activePathId state entirely and use isPathActive() everywhere, or sync it properly via useEffect

title: selectedPath state is set but never read
description: The selectedPath state variable is set on line 73 but never used anywhere in the component. This is dead code.
recommendation: Remove the unused selectedPath state variable

title: Channel list has no loading state
description: While certifications show a loading spinner, channels (from allChannelsConfig) have no loading state shown in the modal. Since allChannelsConfig is a static import, this isn't critical, but the UX is inconsistent.
recommendation: N/A - static import, no loading needed. Consider removing cert loading indicator for consistency.

title: No confirmation or feedback when starting a path
description: Clicking 'Start Path' immediately navigates to '/' with no visible confirmation that the path was activated. Users may not realize their path is now active.
recommendation: Show a toast notification confirming the path was started, or navigate to a dedicated 'active path' view

title: Empty state when filter returns no results
description: If filteredPaths.length === 0, the grid shows nothing. There's no empty state message to tell the user why.
recommendation:
Add empty state: {filteredPaths.length === 0 &&

No paths match this filter

}

title: Delete action has no confirmation
description: Clicking delete immediately removes the path with no confirmation dialog. Users might accidentally delete paths.
recommendation: Add a confirmation dialog before deletion: useConfirmDialog or window.confirm()

title: Edit modal missing Cancel button
description: The edit modal footer only has 'Save Changes' button. Users have no obvious way to discard changes without closing the modal.
recommendation: Add a Cancel button next to Save Changes, or make clicking outside the modal cancel (currently closes it, but no visual indicator)

title: Inconsistent tab naming
description: First tab shows 'All Paths' while others show difficulty names ('Beginner', 'Intermediate'). This inconsistency could confuse users.
recommendation: Use 'All' instead of 'All Paths' for consistency, or add descriptive labels to all tabs

title: Filtered lists not memoized
description: filteredChannels and filteredCerts are computed on every render. With large lists, this causes unnecessary recalculations.
recommendation: Use useMemo: const filteredChannels = useMemo(() => allChannelsConfig.filter(...), [searchQuery])

title: Filtered lists not memoized
description: filteredChannels and filteredCerts are computed on every render without memoization.
recommendation: Wrap in useMemo with [searchQuery, certifications] dependencies

title: Duplicate CSS max-height property
description: Line 310 has 'max-h-[90dvh] max-h-[90svh]' - two max-height values where the second overrides the first. This is redundant.
recommendation: Keep only one: max-h-[90dvh] (or remove the duplicate)

title: Same duplicate max-height issue
description: Edit modal also has duplicate max-h-[90dvh] max-h-[90svh] properties.
recommendation: Remove the duplicate max-height declaration

title: Type cast 'as any' bypasses type safety
description: Using 'as any' to cast the tab value defeats TypeScript's type checking and could allow invalid values.
recommendation: Properly type the activeTab state and tab array

title: Mixed design system usage
description: Line 387 uses 'bg-background text-foreground' (semantic tokens) while siblings use 'bg-[var(--gh-*)]' (GitHub CSS variables). This creates visual inconsistencies across light/dark modes.
recommendation: Pick one design system approach and use consistently: either semantic tokens everywhere or CSS variables everywhere

title: Hardcoded font sizes outside design system
description: text-6xl md:text-7xl are hardcoded Tailwind sizes not mapped to design tokens. These won't scale with user font preferences.
recommendation: Create a --text-hero CSS variable or use consistent heading scale from design system

title: Emoji in page title
description: Title contains '🎯' emoji which may render inconsistently across fonts/browsers and isn't accessible
recommendation: Remove emoji and use a styled icon instead, or add aria-hidden='true'

title: Inline modal styles inconsistent with other modals
description: This modal uses bg-black/50 backdrop while MyPath.tsx edit modal uses bg-background/80. They should match.
recommendation: Use consistent backdrop styling across all modals

title: Hover scale transform could cause layout shift
description: hover:scale-[1.02] on the path cards could cause adjacent elements to shift. Consider using transform instead.
recommendation: Use transform scale instead of layout-affecting properties: hover:scale-[1.02] is fine for small cards

title: Curated path channels also show IDs instead of names
description: Same issue as custom paths - curated path channels (path.channels) are displayed as raw IDs like 'system-design' instead of 'System Design'.
recommendation: Use same channel name lookup: allChannelsConfig.find(c => c.id === channel)?.name || channel
